title: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
subhead: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

Live Nation, parent company of events giant Ticketmaster, found guilty of illegal monopoly in the US
subhead: The verdict could have profound repercussions in the concert world, and possible remedies could include breaking up Live Nation and Ticketmaster.

title: Prominent Palestinian human rights advocate barred from entering France
subhead: Shawan Jabarin, the general director of Al-Haq, a West Bank-based NGO that is currently under US sanctions, was scheduled to speak before the European Parliament's human rights committee in Strasbourg this past Tuesday. However, French authorities denied his visa application.

title: At Lyon's Musée des Confluences, the Loeb donation celebrates Mali's living arts
subhead: More than 100 masks, puppets and instruments are displayed in a way that evokes the singing, dancing and movements that once accompanied ceremonies along the Niger River.

title: What is the controversial bill on antisemitism French MPs are debating?
subhead: MP Caroline Yadan has been accused of trying to silence criticism of Israel with her bill, which aims to 'combat new forms of antisemitism.' A petition against the text has gathered over 700,000 signatures.

title: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
subhead: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.
